claim: Verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git detection, artifact root exclusion, and comprehensive test coverage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function implements dual-mode file discovery: git ls-files with fallback to recursive directory walk
evidence.why: Primary evidence for git-backed and non-git project support.

evidence.summary: Artifact root exclusion via allowedWriteRoots filtering: files in artifact roots are omitted from boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Verifies the dispatch artifact root exclusion requirement.

evidence.summary: Test suite covers git-backed (untracked file detection) and non-git (directory walk + artifact exclusion) scenarios
evidence.why: Confirms comprehensive test coverage for both detection modes.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations result in BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker code and force prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Verifies enforcement mechanism for boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Dispatch artifact root directory exists at expected path
evidence.why: Confirms artifact root structure for exclusion logic.

risks: Explorer evidence reports full implementation, but stress-testing with real Claude -p agents in dirty working trees has not been verified
missing: Stress test evidence from real Claude -p agent runs in dirty working trees
Production usage logs showing boundary detection performance under load

result.notes: Explorer evidence is accurate and sufficient for PRD readiness. All 12 evidence records reference actual project files with correct line numbers. No assumptions promoted to facts. Missing items are operational validation gaps, not implementation gaps. The acceptance criteria for git-backed and non-git detection plus artifact exclusion are met by verified implementation.
